rationale: I am keeping the current best quant-aware/quantizer path intact and shifting to a per-step efficiency hypothesis instead. Muon's Newton-Schulz backend has stayed at 5 steps across the run history; dropping the default to 4 should reduce optimizer overhead on the wallclock-limited Mac mini and buy extra updates in the same 600s budget, while still preserving most of the orthogonalization benefit for the matrix-heavy blocks that dominate the model.
title: Four-Step Muon Orthogonalizer
